sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.32 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Prove an analogue of Higman's theorem for the Burnside variety${ B}_n$of groups of odd exponent$n 1$, that is, prove that every recursively presented group of exponent$n$can be embedded in a finitely presented (in~${ B}_n$) group of exponent$n$. { S.\,V.\,Ivanov} It is proved (A.\,Yu.\,Olshanskii, J.~Algebra, 560 (2020), 960--1052).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
